DISCOVERY 302, PLAINTIFF'S MOTION FOR ATTORNEY'S FEES. AT ISSUE IS PLAINTIFFS' REQUEST FOR ATTORNEYS' FEES AGAINST DEFENDANTS' PRIOR COUNSEL, NA'IL BENJAMIN. ON JUNE 29, 2018, UNDER CCP SECTION 473(B), THIS COURT GRANTED DEFENDANTS' MOTION FOR RELIEF FROM THE IMPOSITION OF EVIDENTIARY SANCTIONS. DEFENDANTS' MOTION WAS PREMISED ON AN ERROR MADE BY MR. BENJAMIN, ITS PRIOR COUNSEL. THE COURT FINDS THAT DEFENDANTS ARE ENTITLED TO "REASONABLE COMPENSATORY LEGAL FEES" FROM MR. BENJAMIN UNDER CCP SECTION 473(B) AND AWARDS THEM A COMBINED TOTAL OF $1,500. FEES TO BE PAID TO PLAINTIFFS BY NOVEMBER 1, 2018. COURT WILL PREPARE ORDER. JUDGE PRO TEM: DAVID MCDONALD; CLERK: WILLIAM TRUPEK; NOT REPORTED. (302/JPT)

DISCOVERY 302, DEFENDANTS SEAN KILLEN, JOSEPH NOBLE, ANDREW QUATTRIN AND NEW BERNAL HEIGHTS, INC.'S MOTION FOR RECONSIDERATION (DEFENDANT KILLEN) - MOTION FOR RECONSIDERATION GRANTED UNDER CCP 1008. THE COURT'S PRIOR ORDER EXPLICITLY STATES THAT EVIDENTIARY SANCTIONS WERE REQUIRED BECAUSE NO DOCUMENTS HAD BEEN PRODUCED AND THAT THE APRIL TRIAL DATE WAS QUICKLY APPROACHING. GIVEN THE CONTINUANCE OF THE TRIAL DATE AND THE APPARENT PRODUCTION OF DOCUMENTS, PLAINTIFFS WILL NO LONGER BE PREJUDICED BY DEFENDANTS' CONDUCT. IN ADDITION, DEFENDANTS' OPPOSITION CONTAINS NEW FACTS RELATING TO THE CIRCUMSTANCES WHY NO APPEARANCE WAS MADE AT THE HEARING ON THE MOTION. THE COMBINATION OF THESE NEW FACTS ARE SUFFICIENT FOR RELIEF UNDER CCP 1008. THE ORIGINAL MOTION FOR TERMINATING AND EVIDENTIARY SANCTIONS IS NOW DENIED AS MOOT GIVEN THE PAYMENT OF SANCTIONS AND THE PRODUCTION OF DOCUMENTS. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. JUDGE PRO-TEM: DAVID MCDONALD; CLERK: CYNTHIA HERBERT; REPORTER: SHEILA PHAM, CSR # 13293 =302/JPT

THE COURT'S PRIOR ORDER EXPLICITLY STATES THAT EVIDENTIARY SANCTIONS WERE REQUIRED BECAUSE NO DOCUMENTS HAD BEEN PRODUCED AND THAT THE APRIL TRIAL DATE WAS QUICKLY APPROACHING. GIVEN THE CONTINUANCE OF THE TRIAL DATE AND THE APPARENT PRODUCTION OF DOCUMENTS, PLAINTIFFS WILL NO LONGER BE PREJUDICED BY DEFENDANTS' CONDUCT. IN ADDITION, DEFENDANTS' OPPOSITION CONTAINS NEW FACTS RELATING TO THE CIRCUMSTANCES WHY NO APPEARANCE WAS MADE AT THE HEARING ON THE MOTION. THE COMBINATION OF THESE NEW FACTS ARE SUFFICIENT FOR RELIEF UNDER CCP 1008. THE ORIGINAL MOTION FOR TERMINATING AND EVIDENTIARY SANCTIONS IS NOW DENIED AS MOOT GIVEN THE PAYMENT OF SANCTIONS AND THE PRODUCTION OF DOCUMENTS. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. DISCOVERY 302, DEFENDANTS SEAN KILLEN, JOSEPH NOBLE, ANDREW QUATTRIN, NEW BERNAL HEIGHTS, INC.'S MOTION FOR RECONSIDERATION (DEFENDANT NOBLE) - MOTION FOR RECONSIDERATION GRANTED UNDER CCP 1008. THE COURT'S PRIOR ORDER EXPLICITLY STATES THAT EVIDENTIARY SANCTIONS WERE REQUIRED BECAUSE NO DOCUMENTS HAD BEEN PRODUCED AND THAT THE APRIL TRIAL DATE WAS QUICKLY APPROACHING. GIVEN THE CONTINUANCE OF THE TRIAL DATE AND THE APPARENT PRODUCTION OF DOCUMENTS, PLAINTIFFS WILL NO LONGER BE PREJUDICED BY DEFENDANTS' CONDUCT. IN ADDITION, DEFENDANTS' OPPOSITION CONTAINS NEW FACTS RELATING TO THE CIRCUMSTANCES WHY NO APPEARANCE WAS MADE AT THE HEARING ON THE MOTION. THE COMBINATION OF THESE NEW FACTS ARE SUFFICIENT FOR RELIEF UNDER CCP 1008. THE ORIGINAL MOTION FOR TERMINATING AND EVIDENTIARY SANCTIONS IS NOW DENIED AS MOOT GIVEN THE PAYMENT OF SANCTIONS AND THE PRODUCTION OF DOCUMENTS. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. JUDGE PRO-TEM: DAVID MCDONALD; CLERK: CYNTHIA HERBERT; REPORTER: SHEILA PHAM, CSR # 13293 =302/JPT

Plaintiff'S Motion For Atty'S Fees
Off Calendar Oct-05-2018 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Carlton D. Floyd, of Law Office of Bradford C. Floyd, Telpehone (707) 445-9754, appering for Plaintiff, Sean Devries(CourtCall);
No other appearance.

The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

Plaintiff's Motion For Attorney's Fees

At issue is Plaintiffs' request for attorneys' fees against Defendants' prior counsel, Na'il Benjamin. On June 29, 2018, under CCP section 473(b), this Court granted Defendants' motion for relief from the imposition of evidentiary sanctions. Defendants' motion was premised on an error made by Mr. Benjamin, its prior counsel. The Court finds that Defendants are entitled to "reasonable compensatory legal fees" from Mr. Benjamin under CCP section 473(b) and awards them a combined total of $1,500. Fees to be paid to Plaintiffs by November 1, 2018. Court will prepare order.

MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Atty. Carlton D. Floyd of the Law Office of Bradford C. Floyd, Tel. No. 1-707-445-9754
appearing for the Plaintiffs by telephone appearance;
Atty. Vernon C. Goins II of Goins & Associates, Tel. No. 1-510-663-3700 appearing
for the Defendants;
Sheila Pham, Tel. No. 1-415-517-5439, Court Reporter.

RULING - The Court adopted its tentative ruling. DEFENDANTS SEAN KILLEN, JOSEPH NOBLE, ANDREW QUATTRIN, NEW BERNAL HEIGHTS, INC.'S Motion For Reconsideration (Defendant Quattrin)- Motion for reconsideration granted under CCP 1008. The Court's prior order explicitly states that evidentiary sanctions were required because no documents had been produced and that the April trial date was quickly approaching. Given the continuance of the trial date and the apparent production of documents, Plaintiffs will no longer be prejudiced by Defendants' conduct. In addition, defendants' opposition contains new facts relating to the circumstances why no appearance was made at the hearing on the motion. The combination of these new facts are sufficient for relief under CCP 1008. The original motion for terminating and evidentiary sanctions is now denied as moot given the payment of sanctions and the production of documents. Order signed in open court. Judge Pro-tem: David Mcdonald; Clerk: Cynthia Herbert; Reporter: Sheila Pham, CSR # 13293 =302/jpt
